mapping.a_element: nonthermal steady state reached after an interaction quench
mapping.b_element: nonequilibrium stationary state of a driven many-particle system

mapping.a_element: generalized Gibbs ensemble describing the final steady state
mapping.b_element: Sinai-Ruelle-Bowen (SRB) distribution describing the stationary state

brief_justification: Both only share the generic problem of finding a statistical ensemble for a nonequilibrium stationary state; the machinery differs and is in fact opposite. Paper A's steady state is described by a generalized Gibbs ensemble arising from the conserved quantities of an (integrable) quantum quench via non-equilibrium DMFT, whereas paper B invokes the SRB measure of a hyperbolic, strongly chaotic deterministic dynamical system, so no role-for-role correspondence between the two mechanisms holds.
